algorithm: The new algorithm modifies edge distances by implementing an exponential adjustment based on the relative entropy of edge usage frequency, seeking to promote diversity by amplifying or diminishing distances in relation to their divergence from uniform utilization.
code:
import numpy as np

# Common backbone idea: Adjust edge distances based on usage metrics to encourage more balanced usage across edges.

# {The new algorithm modifies edge distances by implementing an exponential adjustment based on the relative entropy of edge usage frequency, seeking to promote diversity by amplifying or diminishing distances in relation to their divergence from uniform utilization.}

def update_edge_distance(edge_distance, local_opt_tours, edge_n_used):
    num_edges = np.prod(edge_distance.shape)
    
    total_usage = np.sum(edge_n_used)
    usage_probabilities = edge_n_used / (total_usage + 1e-5)
    uniform_probabilities = np.ones_like(edge_n_used) / num_edges
    
    entropy_divergence = usage_probabilities * np.log((usage_probabilities + 1e-5) / (uniform_probabilities + 1e-5))
    relative_entropy = np.sum(entropy_divergence)
    
    initial_factor = 0.3
    
    updated_edge_distance = np.copy(edge_distance)
    
    for i in range(edge_distance.shape[0]):
        for j in range(edge_distance.shape[1]):
            deviation_factor = np.exp(relative_entropy * (usage_probabilities[i, j] - uniform_probabilities[i, j]))
            adjustment_value = initial_factor * (deviation_factor - 1)
            
            if usage_probabilities[i, j] < uniform_probabilities[i, j]:
                updated_edge_distance[i, j] = max(0, updated_edge_distance[i, j] - adjustment_value)
            else:
                updated_edge_distance[i, j] += adjustment_value

    return updated_edge_distance
